meaning: vt.浣垮叿浣撳寲,浣挎湁褰㈠寲
examples: Together, let us concretize the constructive partnership between China and South Africa!
Metaphor is one kind of means to make the abstract idea concretize in vocabulary teaching.
The report will, I believe, help concretize my commitment to primary health care, while giving policymakers a realistic assessment of what can be achieved and how it can be done.
To concretize what EMTALA does to a healthcare facility, transpose the law to the restaurant setting.
They merely concretize and then expand our experience of life.
